RONDO Records / Est. 2026 Make music physical

RONDO

Music, made visible

A vinyl-soul speaker that turns your streaming library into a record you can watch turn.

Join the first run
/ the quiet / the return / the object / the display / your room / the run
IThe quiet

We traded the sleeve for a thumbnail.

The ritual for a tap.

Ten thousand albums in our pocket,

and not one of them in the room.

Act II / The return 33⅓ rpm

Press play.

The disc turns.

The art breathes.

The words rise.

RONDO makes your streaming library something you can see across the room.

IIIThe object
01 / fabric

Acoustic textile

One piece of speaker-grade weave wrapped over the face, so sound passes through and nothing else does.

Knit over aluminium frame
02 / walnut

Turned walnut

Three legs, each turned from solid walnut and tapered by hand, so the record floats just off the floor.

Solid American walnut
03 / glass

Anti-glare glass

A circular pane that reads like paper in daylight and like a window into the music after dark.

Bonded optical glass
04 / sound

Room-filling sound

Tuned to sit in a room the way a band does, present without shouting, warm without going soft.

Coaxial hi-fi driver
The living display

  • 01 Cover art, in motion

    Every track brings its own world to the glass and lets it drift.

  • 02 Lyrics, line by line

    The words arrive in time, so the whole room can read along.

  • 03 A disc that spins

    A record that actually turns, because watching it is half the point.

  • 04 Or simply the time

    When the music stops, a quiet clock takes the stage.

Furniture that
plays music.

And music that became furniture

Make music physical again  /  Make music physical again  /  Make music physical again  /  Make music physical again  / 
IVThe run

This was never
just a speaker.

It is a small stand against the disappearing of music, against sound with no shape and no place to live. The first run is small on purpose.

14.2K
following the build
500
units, first pressing
9
display modes
1
reason: make it real

For the ones who still sit and listen
